name: Talysh
notes: Talysh (tolışi), a Northwestern Iranian language of the Caspian littoral in Azerbaijan and Iran. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Talysh is written in THREE scripts with no single dominant norm — an Azerbaijani-style Latin alphabet (used by the tly Wikipedia), a Cyrillic alphabet, and a Perso-Arabic script (an abjad) in Iran — and the vowel systems assumed by the Latin and Cyrillic norms differ. No letter-to-phoneme map is claimed; a future spec should target the Latin orthography and state that explicitly as its input contract.
